articles.title: U.S. and Iran Head to Pakistan: A Last-Ditch Effort for Peace?
articles.summary: An American delegation is flying to Pakistan for talks with Iran, aiming to end the two-month war. The key issue is the Strait of Hormuz, where shipping is nearly frozen. Iran's foreign minister is already in Islamabad but says no meeting is planned with the U.S. The White House claims Iran pushed for talks. The U.S. has three aircraft carriers in the region. New sanctions target Iran and Chinese firms. A ceasefire has held for three weeks, but tensions remain high.

articles.title: Appeals Court Strikes Down Trump's Asylum Ban: A Major Legal Blow
articles.summary: A federal appeals court ruled that President Trump's executive order suspending asylum at the southern border is illegal. The three-judge panel said immigration laws guarantee the right to apply for asylum, and the president cannot override them. The White House called the ruling "unsurprising" and blamed political judges. The administration can appeal to the Supreme Court. Advocates hailed the decision as a victory for people fleeing danger. The ruling doesn't immediately change border policy due to a prior pause.

articles.title: DOJ Drops Probe into Fed Chair Powell, Clearing Path for New Leadership
articles.summary: The Justice Department has dropped its criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell over cost overruns in Fed building renovations. The probe had been criticized as a political pressure tactic by the Trump administration. U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro will instead refer the matter to the Fed's inspector general, who previously found no wrongdoing. The move removes a key obstacle to Senate confirmation of Kevin Warsh as the next Fed chair. Powell's term expires next month, but he may remain on the board.
